0.q: photosynthetic characteristics such as net photosynthetic rate (Pn), stomatal conductance (gs), intercellular CO2 (Ci), and chlorophyll (Chl) content were significantly higher in transgenic lines under salinity stress as compared to control plants

overexpression of PDH45 gene in an elite indica rice variety Pusa Basmati 1 enhances the salinity stress tolerance by improving the growth, photosynthesis and antioxidant machinery of the transgenic rice.
Photosynthetic parameters also showed significant increase in PDH45 and PDH45+EPSPS overexpressing transgenic plants in comparison to WT, VC and EPSPS transgenic plants under salinity stress.
A significantly higher chlorophyll content was retained by the marker-free transgenic indicating a robust photosynthetic capacities of the transgenics towards salinity stress tolerance.
when PN, gs, and Ci parameters were measured at the S4 stage, significant enhancement was registered for both pdh and suv lines as compared to WT.
Overexpression of p68 ... confers enhanced tolerances to salinity stress by improving the growth, photosynthesis and antioxidant machinery.

1.q: The activities of antioxidant enzymes, such as catalase (CAT), ascorbate peroxidase (APX), glutathione reductase (GR), and guaiacol peroxidase (GPX), were significantly higher in transgenic lines... keeping the oxidative stress burden (MDA and H2O2) lesser
The marker-free transgenic rice lines showed significantly reduced levels of H2O2 in the leaves... these plants could scavenge ROS-mediated cellular damages under salinity stress

The activities of the components of enzymatic antioxidant machinery were observed to be higher in the transgenic plants indicating the presence of an efficient antioxidant defense system which helps to cope with the stress-induced oxidative-damages.

The activities of antioxidant enzymes viz. SOD, APX, GPX and GR were significantly higher in transgenics; suggesting the existence of an efficient antioxidant defence system to cope with salinity induced-oxidative damage.

4.q: The transgenic plants show better tissue compartmentalization of Na+ and higher ROS scavenging activity which probably results in lower membrane damage.

5.q: PsLecRLK overexpression in rice provides tolerance against salinity stress and cause upregulation of SOS1 pathway genes, which are responsible for extrusion of excess Na+ ion under stress condition.

6.q: Overexpression of PsSEO-F1 tobacco plants confers salinity tolerance by alleviating ionic toxicity and increased ROS scavenging activity which probably results in reduced membrane damage.

7.q: PDH45 and PDH45+EPSPS synergistically modulate the jasmonic acid and salicylic acid mediated signaling pathways for combating salinity stress.

9.q: ROS accumulation was remarkably regulated by the overexpression of pea p68 under salinity stress conditions, as shown from TBARS content, electrolyte leakage, hydrogen peroxide accumulation and 8-OHdG content and antioxidant enzyme activities.

10.q: These results confirm that the full-length pea p68 is able to self-interact in the yeast two-hybrid system.

12.q: The overexpression of PsSEO-F1 modulates the expression of various transporters involved in maintaining cellular homeostasis under abiotic stress conditions.

13.q: PDH45 overexpression ... confers salinity tolerance by improving the photosynthesis and antioxidant machinery; The activities of antioxidant enzymes ... were significantly higher in transgenics.

14.q: PsGa1 interacts with Gb, PLCd, the C2 domain of PLCd and PsGPCR, but not with PsGc1 or PsGa1 itself; PsGb interacts with Ga1, PsGc1, PLCd and PsGPCR

17.q: PsGa1 interacts with Gb, PLCd, the C2 domain of PLCd and PsGPCR... The pea GPCR protein was found to form oligomers by self-interacting, and also interacted with all three subunits of G-proteins

18.q: PsGa1 interacts with Gb, PLCd, the C2 domain of PLCd and PsGPCR... The pea GPCR protein was found to form oligomers by self-interacting, and also interacted with all three subunits of G-proteins

19.q: Transgenic tobacco plants constitutively over-expressing Ga showed tolerance to salinity and heat... these findings provide direct evidence for a novel role of Ga and Gb subunits in abiotic stress tolerance.

20.q: Recombinant CaARP protein expressed in bacteria and plant displayed aldo/keto reductase (AKR) activity with an array of substrates including physiologically toxic aldehydes malondialdehyde (MDA) and methylglyoxal (MG).

21.q: PsMAPK is therefore functional as a MAPK in this osmosensing signal transduction pathway.

22.q: we describe the cloning and functional characterization of the three subunits of heterotrimeric G-proteins and a GPCR from pea

23.q: possible cross-talk between PLC- and G-protein-mediated signaling pathways

24.q: The MCMs provide DNA unwinding function during the DNA replication... the MCM6 transcript is upregulated in pea plant in response to high salinity and cold stress.

25.q: The GTPase activity of the Ga subunit increased after interaction with PLCd.

26.q: PsSEO-F1-overexpressing transgenic tobacco showed enhanced salinity stress tolerance by maintaining cellular ion homeostasis and modulating ROS-scavenging pathway.
